title: The Contract Board
lineage: MINIMALIST SPACE LOGISTICS · R.4 — the faction board

scenes.narration: Minimalist Space Logistics. The Contract Board. A quiet board is not the quest list drying up. It's a war going broke, telling you in the only language it has.

scenes.headline: The board is a live X-ray of a war you never see.
scenes.body: You run one hauler. The faction posts the work. Every contract is a coin the treasury could spare and a hole at the front it needs plugged. Read as a whole, the board is the faction AI's entire external surface.
scenes.narration: You run one hauler, and the faction posts the work. Every contract is a coin the treasury could spare and a hole at the front it needs plugged. Read as a whole, the board is a live X-ray of a war you never see directly.

scenes.headline: Generosity is the treasury, expressed as offers.
scenes.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 1200 540" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" font-family="monospace"><rect x="80" y="60" width="320" height="110" rx="10" fill="#0a0c12" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="240" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" fill="#f59e0b">TREASURY</text><text x="240" y="138" text-anchor="middle" font-size="18" fill="#c4c9d8">one number</text><line x1="400" y1="115" x2="540" y2="115" stroke="#c4c9d8" stroke-width="2"/><text x="470" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="18" fill="#c4c9d8">greedy fill</text><rect x="540" y="40" width="560" height="460" rx="10" fill="#0a0c12" stroke="#2a3146" stroke-width="2"/><text x="820" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" fill="#9aa1b6">THE BOARD</text><rect x="570" y="95" width="500" height="50" rx="6" fill="#141826" stroke="#f59e0b"/><text x="600" y="126" font-size="18" fill="#f59e0b">Munitions</text><text x="1040" y="126" text-anchor="end" font-size="18" fill="#f59e0b">1600 cr</text><rect x="570" y="160" width="500" height="50" rx="6" fill="#141826" stroke="#60a5fa"/><text x="600" y="191" font-size="18" fill="#60a5fa">Drone cores</text><text x="1040" y="191" text-anchor="end" font-size="18" fill="#60a5fa">1400 cr</text><rect x="570" y="225" width="500" height="50" rx="6" fill="#141826" stroke="#34d399"/><text x="600" y="256" font-size="18" fill="#34d399">Rations</text><text x="1040" y="256" text-anchor="end" font-size="18" fill="#34d399">620 cr</text><rect x="570" y="290" width="500" height="50" rx="6" fill="#141826" stroke="#2a3146"/><text x="600" y="321" font-size="18" fill="#9aa1b6">Spare parts</text><text x="1040" y="321" text-anchor="end" font-size="18" fill="#9aa1b6">760 cr</text><text x="820" y="400" text-anchor="middle" font-size="17" fill="#9aa1b6">funded only while the slice</text><text x="820" y="428" text-anchor="middle" font-size="17" fill="#9aa1b6">above the floor can pay</text></svg>
scenes.narration: Nobody hand-authors this list. Each cycle the faction does one cheap thing: a budget-constrained greedy fill. A fat treasury floods the board; a thin one posts a short, lean, military-first list. Generosity is the treasury, drawn as offers.

scenes.headline: A thinning, military-skewing board is an early-warning system.
scenes.body: As the treasury drains, the board fails in order: first the generals get cut, then payouts shrink, then the count collapses. All military, all small, all urgent means a faction in its last stand.
scenes.narration: Here's the read the page is built to teach. As a run wears on, the board fails in a learnable order: generals get cut, payouts shrink, the count collapses. All military, all small, all urgent is a faction in its last stand.

scenes.headline: The danger is never in the payout.
bullets: Same clock — contracts expire on the very clock that overruns systems.
Overrun voids it — if the drop-off falls before you arrive, the job pays nothing.
Unpriced risk — no premium for a near-front run; the only tell is the danger marker's geometry.
Deliberate — pricing the danger in would do the reading for you.
scenes.narration: Contracts expire on the same clock that overruns systems. If a drop-off is overrun before you arrive, the contract is voided and pays nothing. The danger is never priced into the payout; you read it in the geometry, not the number.

scenes.headline: Drive it yourself.
bullets: Play / Step / Reset — run the deterministic tick, single-step it, or reset to seed 4174.
The dials — starting treasury, war intensity, spendable floor, war-bond refill, military weighting, front advance.
The scrub bar — drag through the whole stored run; rewind any time.
Scenarios — Drain the treasury, Fat war chest, and Front collapse.
scenes.narration: Drive it yourself. Press Play to run the tick, or Step through it. Work the dials: starting treasury, war intensity, the spendable floor. Drag the scrub bar to rewind the whole run, then try the scenarios, drain the treasury or trigger a front collapse.

scenes.headline: The floor keeps a losing war from becoming unwinnable-and-boring.
scenes.body: If generosity were purely the treasury, a losing war could empty the board to zero income exactly when you need a comeback. The spendable fraction is a floor: a slice the faction dips into to keep a thin, real board of military jobs alive.
scenes.narration: If generosity were purely the treasury, a losing war could starve you of all income just when you need a comeback. The spendable floor fixes that: a slice the faction keeps a minimum board alive on. It's the difference between hard and unwinnable.

scenes.headline: The Contract Board
scenes.body: A quiet board is a war going broke · deterministic, seeded, self-contained, no network.
scenes.narration: The board is a live histogram of what the war can afford to want. A quiet board is a war going broke. Deterministic, seeded, self-contained.
